config.model: FusionModel (3B StructureSequenceEncoder + 3A ChartCNN, late-fused by a 2->8->1 MLP)
config.fusion: late (each branch -> scalar score; MLP over [score_3b, score_3a])
config.purge: P3-A3 year-boundary embargo: dropped 489 fit samples whose 21d label crossed into an OOS year

eval.eval_method: within-train fit/OOS year-block split with P3-A3 fit→OOS year-boundary purge embargo; per-OOS-date cross-sectional Spearman rank-IC of fused score vs 21d close-to-close forward return; paired t-test of per-date IC vs baseline
eval.turnover_penalty: monthly top-8 name turnover = 0.768; implied cost drag ~2.76%/yr at 30bp/side

root_cause: 3C late-fusion OOS rank-IC is not significantly different from the 126d momentum baseline (paired t-test p=0.177). The two fused branches both re-encode the same price-window trend the momentum factor already summarizes — consistent with the Phase 2A family-T redundancy finding and the 3B/3A negative results. Config-scoped: THIS fusion topology, THESE branch encoders, THIS within-train sample — not a blanket verdict on chart-native fusion. IMPORTANT — UNDERFIT CONFOUND: train MSE only fell 0.999->0.808 on a unit-variance z-scored target, i.e. the fused model explained <19% of even the TRAINING signal. The weak OOS result is therefore confounded with underfitting and is NOT evidence that late fusion cannot work — config-scoped diagnostic, not a verdict.
notes: Phase 3 chart-native late-fusion attempt. Within-train fit/OOS split — no validation/sealed data read. Date-subsampled (stride 3) for RAM, aligned (seg,img) per sample. ref_3b/3a IC are the recorded P3·R2/P3·R4 single-branch OOS rank-IC on the same protocol.
